title: myosotis palustris subsp. lingulata
intro: Myosotis palustris subsp. lingulata, also known as marsh forget-me-not, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae) that is native to Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat various ailments, such as skin conditions, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. The herbal preparations of marsh forget-me-not typically involve making teas, tinctures, or infusions from the leaves and flowers of the plant.
